---
title: "[YOUR PROJECT TITLE]"
author: "[YOUR NAME]"
date: today
date-format: iso
abstract: |
  [One-paragraph abstract: question, data, method, headline result, contribution.]
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: true
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    keep-tex: true
    fig-pos: "H"
bibliography: ../references.bib
csl: https://www.zotero.org/styles/american-economic-review
---

<!--
================================================================================
  HOW TO USE THIS TEMPLATE
================================================================================

  This is a Quarto report driven by the Stata Quarto engine. The pipeline
  produces all tables (output/tables/*.tex / *.csv) and figures
  (output/figures/*.pdf / *.png) BEFORE this report renders. The report
  *includes* those artifacts; it never re-runs analysis.

  Render with:
      quarto render reports/analysis_report.qmd
  or use the /render-report skill.

  Numerical claims in the prose MUST trace to a logs/ line; the log-validator
  agent enforces this before committing.

================================================================================
-->

## Introduction

[Motivate the question. ~3 paragraphs. End with the paper's contribution and
roadmap.]

## Data {#sec-data}

[Describe the data sources, sample period, unit of observation, and any
restrictions applied to construct the analysis sample. Reference the data
dictionary at `data/README.md` for variable details.]

**Sample size after restrictions:** [N from logs/02_construct_sample.log,
quote the line].

```{=html}
<!-- Include the descriptive-statistics table produced by Stata -->
```
::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
{{< include ../output/tables/descriptive_stats.tex >}}
:::

## Identification {#sec-identification}

[State the exact estimand, the identifying assumption, and the evidence for it.
Cite key references.]

We estimate the average treatment effect on the treated (ATT) under the
parallel-trends assumption [@callawaysantanna2021]. Pre-trend evidence is
shown in @fig-eventstudy.

## Empirical Strategy {#sec-strategy}

[Lay out the regression specification. Use math display for the equation.
Discuss SE choice and FE structure.]

We estimate

$$
y_{it} = \alpha_i + \gamma_t + \beta \cdot D_{it} + X_{it}'\theta + \varepsilon_{it}
$$ {#eq-main}

where $D_{it}$ is the treatment indicator and $X_{it}$ is a vector of controls.
Standard errors are clustered at [unit level] following [@bertrandduflomullainathan2004].

## Results {#sec-results}

The headline ATT is reported in @tbl-main.

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
{{< include ../output/tables/main_regression.tex >}}
:::

[Discuss the magnitude, sign, and statistical significance. Cite the log
line that produced the headline number, e.g., `logs/03_analysis_main_regression.log:412`.]

![Event-study estimates with 95% CIs](../output/figures/event_study.pdf){#fig-eventstudy width=80%}

## Robustness {#sec-robustness}

[Walk through alternate specifications. Reference each in turn.]

## Conclusion {#sec-conclusion}

[Restate the result, the assumption it relies on, and what it implies.]

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
